<!--
Thanks for contributing! Keep PRs focused — one logical change per PR.
Fill in every section; PRs with an empty "Linked issue / design" or an
unchecked DCO box will be asked to update before review.
-->

## What this changes

A short description of the change and the behavior it affects.

## Linked issue / design (required)

<!-- A PR should trace to a tracked decision. Link at least one. -->

- Closes #
- Or design reference: a tracked issue, roadmap item, or design doc under `docs/`

## Type of change

- [ ] Bug fix (no behavior change beyond the fix)
- [ ] Feature / new behavior
- [ ] Refactor (no observable behavior change)
- [ ] Docs only
- [ ] Build / CI / chore

## Docs & tests (kept in sync with code)

<!-- See CONTRIBUTING.md. Docs and tests are part of the same change as
the code they describe — not a follow-up. -->

- [ ] Unit tests added/updated for new or changed observable behavior (or N/A — pure refactor)
- [ ] Integration / e2e tests updated if a contract, signature, or schema changed (or N/A)
- [ ] Docs updated for any surface this touches (`README.md`, `docs/`, env-var tables, the privacy/data-handling references) — or a note below on why none were needed
- [ ] `CHANGELOG.md` updated under `[Unreleased]` if this is user- or operator-visible

## Verification

How did you verify this? (commands run + result — e.g. `make check-all`, `go test ./...`, `helm lint`)

```
(paste verification output or commands)
```

## Developer Certificate of Origin (DCO)

- [ ] **I certify the [DCO](https://developercertificate.org/) and have signed off every commit** (`git commit -s`).

<!--
Sign-off adds a `Signed-off-by: Name <email>` trailer to each commit,
asserting you have the right to submit the work under the project license.
Amend an existing commit with: git commit --amend -s
-->
